description: First customer fixture for Nail Content Ekip: The Claw custom nail studio in Budapest.

customer.name: The Claw
customer.source: BookNow The Claw public site and design notes
customer.positioning: Reference-led custom nails: bring the image or mood, then shape, finish, color, and detail become a booked set.

sourceIntent.targetOutcome: Generate a Drop that turns real The Claw work photos into a controlled editorial Contact Sheet while preserving nail shape, finish, color truth, and reference-led brand attitude.

productLanguage.workSet: The Claw real client nail photos
productLanguage.brief: Reference-led custom nails in a black grit editorial world. The nails stay product-true: sharp shape, high finish, visible texture, believable skin, and enough surrounding attitude to feel like The Claw.
productLanguage.edit: A post-ready Drop with close proof, angled hand poses, detail crops, and one booking-friendly vertical frame.

nailTruth.description: How strongly the output must preserve nail shape, length, finish, color, and client-hand believability from the Work Set.

brandTransferStrength.description: How strongly the output borrows The Claw's black grit, lacquer-red, torn-paper, tactile, reference-led visual language.

compositionInvention.description: How much the model may improve hand pose, crop, camera angle, and editorial framing instead of preserving the uploaded photo composition.

bookingUsefulness.description: How directly the final Drop helps a prospective client understand the set, save the reference, or book a similar service.

sourcePhotoDependence.description: How much the final result should resemble the uploaded photos beyond the nail work truth.

variantMatrix.description: Default The Claw behavior: preserve nail truth strictly, transfer brand strongly, and invent stronger editorial framing for booking-ready proof.
Conservative variant for result accuracy: strongest preservation of nail and hand details with less aggressive scene invention.
More authored social content: The Claw visual language gets louder while nail finish and hand believability remain hard gates.

acceptanceGates: Nails keep believable shape, color, finish, and texture.
Hands look real, not plastic, airbrushed, or warped.
The image reads as The Claw: black grit, sharp tactile attitude, reference-led custom work.
The Drop can support booking confidence, not just aesthetic browsing.
No output looks like generic stock nail salon content.

versioningPolicy.outputs: saved under the variant and run that produced them
